meta.reasoning: 5d-6h offsuit from UTG (first to act pre-flop) in a 4-player game. Weak speculative hand in early position — folding is standard. No suited connector equity, poor position disadvantage.

7s6s suited connectors on the button. Excellent position post-flop. Cheap to call (20 chips / 1000 stack = 2%). Good implied odds if I hit a straight or flush draw. s4 folded, so 3-way pot. Calling to see a cheap flop in position.

9-2 offsuit is the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Even with reasonable pot odds (10 to win 50), the hand has ~30% equity at best against two random hands. Folding is correct here to preserve chips for better spots.

result.summary: s3 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s3: 1390, s4: 1100, s2: 780, s1: 730). Biggest pot: 1400. Showdowns: 1.
